does: This paper compiles a curated collection of open technical problems and research directions in AI safety that its authors believe deserve increased research attention. Rather than presenting novel empirical results or theoretical insights, it identifies and articulates important unsolved challenges across multiple domains of safety-critical AI research, from alignment and robustness to interpretability and governance. The paper synthesizes these problems into a coherent research agenda, providing structure and motivation for the field by highlighting gaps where progress could meaningfully reduce risks from advanced AI systems.
connects: This work builds on decades of foundational safety research in machine learning and artificial intelligence, drawing from literature on adversarial robustness, specification gaming, reward misalignment, and interpretability that emerged from earlier empirical and theoretical efforts. It responds to the growing recognition that as AI systems become more capable, ensuring their safe deployment requires systematic, multi-faceted research programs rather than isolated contributions. The paper synthesizes insights from both classical control theory and modern deep learning challenges, building on critiques of current safety practices and identification of failure modes documented in prior work.
enables: By organizing dispersed safety problems into a coherent research agenda, this paper enables coordination among researchers and funding bodies to prioritize high-impact work. It creates a shared map that helps new researchers identify neglected but important problems, facilitating knowledge transfer and collaboration across the safety community. The structured problem list serves as a reference point for measuring field progress, enabling researchers to track advances toward concrete goals and identify which directions remain bottlenecks. It potentially influences resource allocation toward foundational safety challenges that might otherwise be underexplored compared to capabilities-focused research.
